deploy_target.note: Two tasks: generate_omop_data (writes the 6 OMOP tables; deterministic seed=42, safe to re-run) and land_trial_feed (a LONG-RUNNING, presenter-controlled LIVE simulator that drops clinical-trials JSON files into the trial_landing Volume over time — a ~50-min scripted opening act then an indefinite heartbeat). This is the shared foundation every track (Governance, Data Engineering, ML) builds on. Deploy from a web terminal — never from inside Genie Code. Presenter control: start = run the job (or Repair-run just land_trial_feed); pause = cancel the run (only the feed is still running); restart = Run now, or Repair-run selecting only land_trial_feed. Because land_trial_feed runs until cancelled, this job run stays in RUNNING state by design.

toggle.note: yes = run the generator into client_catalog.client_schema (synthetic, no PHI). no = skip generation; downstream tracks read the 6 OMOP tables from source_catalog.source_schema (real curated_omop.omop). The 6 OMOP table names are identical in both modes, so no query changes are needed. The trials JSON feed is net-new synthetic data and is always generated by land_trial_feed regardless of the toggle.

tables.name: person
tables.role: One row per patient. Key columns: person_id (PK), year_of_birth, gender_source_value, birth_date.

tables.name: condition_occurrence
tables.role: Breast cancer diagnoses. condition_source_value = 'Malignant neoplasm of breast' identifies breast cancer patients.

tables.name: measurement
tables.role: Structured biomarker results (ER/PR/HER2). measurement_source_value identifies the marker; value_source_value = 'Positive'/'Negative'. Absent for notes-only patients (person_ids 181-240).

tables.name: observation
tables.role: Menopausal status (observation_source_value='Menopausal status') and AJCC tumor stage (observation_source_value='AJCC stage'). Each patient gets both observation types.

tables.name: drug_exposure
tables.role: Prior therapy. drug_source_value='Trastuzumab' or 'Pertuzumab' flags prior anti-HER2 therapy — a disqualifier for Trial A (person_ids 21-30 are ineligible controls).

tables.name: note
tables.role: Free-text pathology reports in note_text. Contains biomarker phrasing for both-agree patients (1-180) and notes-only patients (181-240); no biomarker phrasing for structured-only patients (241-300). The NLP value story: ~60 patients (181-240) are invisible to structured queries but discoverable via note_text extraction.

trials_feed.produced_by: src/data_generation/generate_trial_feed.py (land_trial_feed task — a LIVE simulator, not a static drop)
trials_feed.file_pattern: trials_NNN_<label>.json (one unique newline-delimited JSON file per drop, so Auto Loader ingests each incrementally)

phases.opening_act: ~50 min at speed=1.0, 11 scripted drops in order: clean Trials A/B/C, two net-new clean trials (D/E), an additive schema-evolution (Trial A re-lands with min_ecog), then the bad records — a record missing trial_id, a malformed JSON line, a wrong-typed field (min_age_years:'eighteen'), and a conflicting re-land of Trial B. A clean Trial F lands mid-way so good rows keep flowing while bad ones quarantine.
phases.heartbeat: After the opening act, an indefinite heartbeat re-lands an existing trial every --heartbeat-seconds (default 300) with a bumped feed_version + fresh load_ts (all clean latest-version data) so the incremental stream stays alive until the run is cancelled.

trials_feed.record_shape: Each record carries trial_id, title, status, phase, a nested eligibility object, eligibility_text, plus feed_version and load_ts. load_ts is stamped at drop time and is the dedup key (latest-wins per trial_id).
trials_feed.tuning: --speed divides all delays (speed=6 ≈ 8-min dry run); --heartbeat-seconds sets the post-opening cadence; --reset clears the Volume first; --max-runtime-min self-stops. Pass via 'Run now with different parameters'.
trials_feed.description: A live clinical-trials registry feed landed as unique FILES in a Unity Catalog Volume (not a finished table). One shared producer, many independent consumers: each Data Engineering team points its own Auto Loader (cloudFiles) + checkpoint at this Volume and writes bronze/silver into its own schema. The DE track lands it in a schema-stable VARIANT bronze, flattens to silver_trial_criteria (the eligibility contract the ML pre-screen joins against), and routes bad records to a quarantine table.
trials_feed.note: Adding a trial is dropping a file, not a code change. This feed is net-new synthetic data; it is NOT governed by the synthetic/real toggle — it always runs.

trial_a.name: HER2-Positive Breast Cancer
trial_a.ineligible_reason: Prior anti-HER2 therapy (Trastuzumab and/or Pertuzumab in drug_exposure)

both_agree.description: Structured AND note biomarkers present. NLP ground truth for MLflow evaluation.

notes_only.description: Biomarker status in note_text ONLY — invisible to structured queries. The NLP value story.

structured_only.description: Structured measurement rows only; note_text has no biomarker phrasing.

gold.note: The foundation has no SDP pipeline — all 6 OMOP tables are written directly by the generator and behave as analytics-ready OMOP CDM tables. Downstream kits build bronze/silver/gold on top.

dependency_map.note: land_trial_feed task streams unique trials_NNN_*.json files (live simulator, presenter-controlled) into the shared Volume
Deep-cloned / governed by the Governance track
DE reads the 6 OMOP tables read-only as its ingest source
ML runs ai_query NLP extraction over note_text
DE ingests the JSON feed into a VARIANT bronze then silver_trial_criteria

grain_constraints.note: One row per patient, 300 rows total
Up to 3 rows per patient (ER, PR, HER2); absent for notes-only group (181-240)
2 rows per patient: menopausal status + AJCC stage

source_inputs.description: Faker-based Python scripts. generate_omop_data.py writes all 6 OMOP tables (argv[1]=catalog, argv[2]=schema, seed=42 for determinism); generate_trial_feed.py lands the nested trials JSON feed into the trial_landing Volume.
source_inputs.active_when: run_with_synthetic_data = yes (OMOP tables). The trials feed is generated regardless of the toggle.

source_inputs.description: Customer's real OMOP CDM tables. Must have identical schema to the synthetic tables (exact FH curated_omop.omop column names and types). Only the 6 OMOP tables are toggled; the trials feed is always synthetic.

verify_queries.description: Row counts for all 6 OMOP tables — all must be > 0 after the generate_omop_data task
verify_queries.sql: SELECT 'person' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.person UNION ALL SELECT 'condition_occurrence', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.condition_occurrence UNION ALL SELECT 'measurement', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement UNION ALL SELECT 'observation', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.observation UNION ALL SELECT 'drug_exposure', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.drug_exposure UNION ALL SELECT 'note', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note ORDER BY tbl
verify_queries.expected_synthetic: person=300, condition_occurrence=300, measurement=720, observation=720, drug_exposure=383, note=265

verify_queries.description: The land_trial_feed simulator is streaming files into the trial_landing Volume. The count GROWS as the run continues (this is a live feed, not a fixed set).
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n_lines FROM read_files('/Volumes/${client_catalog}/${client_schema}/trial_landing/trial_catalog/trials_*.json', format => 'text')
verify_queries.expected_synthetic: >= 1 shortly after the run starts, rising to ~11+ once the opening act completes and continuing to climb on the heartbeat

verify_queries.description: Patients with pathology notes but no structured biomarker measurements (the NLP value story)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT n.person_id) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note n WHERE n.person_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT person_id FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement WHERE measurement_source_value IN ('HER2/neu','Estrogen receptor','Progesterone receptor'))

lock_targets.reason: OMOP CDM table names (person, condition_occurrence, measurement, observation, drug_exposure, note) are standard. The synth/real toggle works because synthetic and real OMOP tables share identical names. Renaming breaks the toggle and the downstream kits.
Adaptation skill must not modify itself
